result:
Done. The app now has a full per-workspace RBAC system, verified end-to-end against the live database (I left the changes uncommitted).

**Data model** (`src/db.ts`): three new tables â€” `roles` (per-workspace, with an `is_system` flag and case-insensitive unique names so a custom role can't shadow `ADMIN`/`GUEST`), `role_permissions`, and `member_roles` (composite FK onto `workspace_members`, so assignments can only exist for real members and cascade away with them). Multiple roles per member fall out of the join table. A one-time idempotent backfill runs inside the startup migration: it seeds `ADMIN`/`GUEST` for existing workspaces, maps legacy `owner`â†’ADMIN and `member`â†’GUEST, then drops the old `workspace_members.role` column â€” I confirmed this ran correctly against your existing data.

**Semantics** (`src/workspaces/permissions.ts`): four permissions â€” `members.read`, `members.invite`, `roles.manage`, `roles.assign`. `ADMIN` holds every permission implicitly (so it never goes stale when new permissions are added); `GUEST` gets `members.read`. Workspace creators get `ADMIN`; invitation acceptors get `GUEST`. System roles' permissions can't be edited, and the last ADMIN assignment can't be removed (guarded by a workspace row lock against concurrent removals).

**Routes** (`src/workspaces/routes.ts`): every workspace route now goes through `requirePermission`, which keeps the existing 404-for-non-members behavior and returns 403 for members lacking the permission. Inviting changed from "owners only" to `members.invite`. New endpoints:

- `GET /workspaces/:id/roles` â€” list roles with permissions
- `POST /workspaces/:id/roles` â€” create a custom role (409 on duplicate name, reserved names rejected)
- `PUT /workspaces/:id/roles/:roleId/permissions` â€” replace a custom role's permission set
- `POST /workspaces/:id/members/:userId/roles` â€” assign a role (idempotent)
- `DELETE /workspaces/:id/members/:userId/roles/:roleId` â€” remove a role

Member/workspace listings now return a `roles` array instead of the single role. Typecheck is clean, and I smoke-tested the whole flow live: guest denied invite/role-creation (403), custom "Recruiter" role granting invite rights then losing them when its permissions were cleared, system-role edit rejection, last-admin protection (409), and non-member probing (404). The README's workspace section is updated to match.
